name: Joe Venuti
description: American jazz musician and pioneer jazz violinist (1903–1978)
abstract: Giuseppe "Joe" Venuti (September 16, 1903 – August 14, 1978) was an American jazz musician and pioneer jazz violinist. Considered the father of jazz violin, he pioneered the use of string instruments in jazz along with the guitarist Eddie Lang, a friend since childhood.

concerts.label: Dick Schory and His Percussion Pops Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Quinn Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Him and Joe Venuti used to hook up there. They were great friends, they were buddies.

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: There's a guy in there, an excellent saxophone player by the name of Joe Venuti and I had known him for sometime and liked him... so they wanted to know if I'd join them and I joined them. I played with the band

about_quotes.speaker: Fred Radke
about_quotes.evidence: Well Joe was my good friend and we played together — I did his last album. Joe and I belonged to an organization together that we spent time together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Fred Radke interviewed by Monk Rowe, Hillsdale, Michigan, November 8, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Al Hall
about_quotes.evidence: I played with all the violinists that -- all the noted violinists through Eddie South and Joe Venuti

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Hyman
about_quotes.evidence: we also did a couple of things as a trio with Joe Venuti — Panama Francis and Venuti and I.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Hyman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: I went with Joe Venuti, immediately when I got out of high school I was with Joe Venuti
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
